fields.description:
# Realign rebar reviews to the three-pass (evidence -> binary-verify -> deterministic-gate) structure

## Context
Industry practice and 2025-2026 research converge: an LLM reviewer should surface cited evidence against a locked rubric; a SEPARATE agent should validate each finding via atomic binary sub-questions; and a DETERMINISTIC policy (not the model) should decide severity and blocking. LLMs are unreliable at holistic severity/confidence judgments, so neither is emitted by the model. This epic builds that shared three-pass framework and migrates rebar's existing reviewers onto it.

## Success Criteria
- All rebar reviews emit the pass-1 evidence contract; a shared pass-2 verifier agent and pass-3 deterministic aggregator exist and are used.
- Severity is computed deterministically from structured attributes; validity/confidence from typed binary sub-answers. No model-emitted severity number, confidence number, or high/med/low rating in the decision path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pass-1 finding contract: `{finding (free text), criteria[] (each in the prompt's locked rubric set), evidence[] (flexible free text: code citations, prose, OR absence rationale), scenarios[] (free text), impact (free text)}`. Criteria is an array. No model severity/confidence.
- [ ] Pass-2 verifier = a separate agent / independent context that re-grounds in the actual code and emits per finding: (a) severity attributes `{prod_impact, debt_impact, blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility}` (coarse ordinals); (b) typed binary sub-answers `{verdict: yes|no|insufficient, evidence_span/reason}`.
- [ ] Binary sub-question set (typed): the ONLY veto is cited-code-reference-accuracy, applied ONLY when a code citation is present (no-op for absence findings / non-code reviews); all others GRADED: is-verifiable (signal), evidence-entails-finding, path-reachable, impact-follows-necessarily, no-viable-alternative-explanation, no-existing-mitigation, severity-claim-justified.
- [ ] Pass-3 deterministic aggregator: conditional veto gate -> graded confidence (weighted fraction / log-odds) -> severity-from-attributes -> `block | advisory` against per-criteria thresholds (project-overridable; defaults start high -> almost all findings advisory while calibration data is gathered). No interactive escalation path (autonomous).
- [ ] Verifier prompt obeys the research rules: atomicity (one proposition per question); independence (do not show the finding's own conclusion when answering each sub-question); verdict-with-citation, NOT verdict-with-proposed-fix; allow "insufficient".
- [ ] Existing code-review reviewers (review_ticket and the code-quality/security/tests reviewers) migrated to the pass-1 contract + shared pass-2/pass-3; findings schema extended; aggregation + citation-resolution + schema-pin tests updated. (completion_verifier is OUT OF SCOPE: not migrated, unchanged.)
- [ ] Rubric sourced from Google eng-practices + Anthropic security taxonomy (locked + versioned); Conventional Comments not adopted.
- [ ] Known limitations recorded: veto-brittleness mitigated by the single conditional veto; decomposition controls validity PRECISION not finding-type RECALL; threshold calibration is explicitly out of scope (separate future work).
